lessons.title: Find the Exchange Sacrifice
lessons.description: Spot when sacrificing a Rook for a minor piece gives you a dominant position.

steps.instruction: Black's Knight on c6 is a key defender. Your Rook can reach c1. But there's a stronger idea: sacrifice material for positional dominance! Play Nxc6!

feedback.correct: You've removed the key defender! After bxc6, Black's pawn structure is ruined with a weak c6 pawn and holes everywhere.
feedback.incorrect: Play Nxc6 - this removes a key defender and ruins Black's pawn structure after bxc6.

steps.instruction: Black recaptured. Now your Bishop on d3 dominates the light squares. What's the most aggressive continuation? Target the weakened kingside!

feedback.correct: Powerful! The Queen joins the attack. With Black's structure ruined and your active pieces, you have a dominating position despite the exchange!
feedback.incorrect: Bring the Queen to the attack! Qh5 or Qg4 targets the weakened kingside.

lessons.successMessage: You spotted the exchange sacrifice! Sometimes ruining the structure is worth more than material.
hints: Look for a way to ruin Black's pawn structure
Nxc6 removes a defender and creates weak pawns
After bxc6, Black has doubled c-pawns and weak squares

lessons.title: Maintain Initiative with Tempo
lessons.description: Practice maintaining the initiative by making threats with every move.

steps.instruction: You have a strong Knight on e5 and active pieces. Maintain initiative! Play Bf4 - develop the last piece with a threat on c7!

feedback.correct: Tempo! You developed with a threat to c7. Black must respond, keeping you in control.
feedback.incorrect: Play Bf4 - develop your last piece while threatening c7.

steps.instruction: Black defended. Now increase the pressure! Play Qh5 - threaten the kingside while maintaining initiative!

feedback.correct: Another tempo move! Qh5 creates kingside threats. Black must keep defending while you keep attacking.
feedback.incorrect: Play Qh5 - keep making threats! The initiative means your opponent has to keep reacting.

steps.instruction: Black weakened the kingside with ...g6. Now retreat the Queen to a new attacking square. Play Qh3 to keep pressure on e6!

feedback.correct: The Queen regroups but maintains pressure. You've weakened g6, now target e6. The initiative is yours!
feedback.incorrect: Play Qh3 - regroup the Queen while targeting e6. Keep the initiative alive!

lessons.successMessage: You maintained the initiative! Key: every move should contain a threat. Never give the opponent time to breathe.
hints: Every move should contain a threat
Develop with tempo (threats on the same move)

lessons.title: Find the Saving Perpetual Check
lessons.description: You're losing! Find the perpetual check to save the game.

steps.instruction: You're down a whole Queen's worth of material (Black Q vs your Q). But Black's King looks exposed on g8. Can you find perpetual check? Start with Qd5+!

feedback.correct: Check! The Queen attacks from d5. After Kh8 or Kf8, you keep checking!
feedback.incorrect: Play Qd5+ - give check aiming for perpetual!

steps.instruction: King goes to h8. Now Qf5! Threaten Qf8# and prepare more checks!

feedback.correct: Keep checking! The King can never escape the checks. This is a DRAW by perpetual check despite being down material!
feedback.incorrect: Keep giving checks! Try Qd8+ or Qe5+ - the King can't escape.

lessons.successMessage: You found the perpetual check! Even in losing positions, always look for drawing resources.
hints: The Queen is amazing at this - it attacks from far away
Even down material, perpetual check saves the game

lessons.title: Build a Fortress
lessons.description: Set up an unbreakable defensive position to draw despite being down material.

steps.instruction: You're down a Rook for a Bishop! But the position is locked. Play Be1 to protect the d4 pawn and set up a fortress!

feedback.correct: The Bishop defends everything! The d4-e3 chain is unbreakable. Black's Rook can't break through because all entry points are covered.
feedback.incorrect: Play Be1 - this creates a fortress by defending d4 and controlling the dark squares.

steps.instruction: Black checks, but your King is safe. Play Kf3 - the King stays near the pawns!

feedback.correct: FORTRESS! The position is a draw. Black's Rook cannot break through the d4-e3-Be1 structure. This is an unbreakable defensive setup!
feedback.incorrect: Play Kf3 - keep the King near the fortress structure.

lessons.successMessage: You built a fortress! Even down material, some positions are simply unbreakable. A great defensive resource!
hints: A fortress is an unbreakable defensive position
The d4-e3 pawn chain can't be attacked
The Bishop defends everything from e1
